corrections.text: Two of the four cited sources are not on The Machine Herald's source allowlist: atalayar.com (used here from the outlet's Opinion section) and european.express. They appear only as supplementary corroboration. Every load-bearing fact in this article — the selection of the Domyn-led EUROPA consortium, the all-24-EU-languages and open-source scope, the 'more than 400 billion parameters' target, the 'up to 2.5% of EuroHPC capacity for one year' compute prize, the 19 June 2026 announcement and 13 February 2026 launch dates, and the Henna Virkkunen quote and title — is independently confirmed verbatim by the two allowlisted European Commission primary sources (digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu).
